title: PREx output (combined)
description: All models PREx emits across `output/graph.json` and `output/brief.json`. Use `Graph` and `Brief` as the two top-level documents.

LineRange.description: Inclusive 1-indexed line range in a file at a given SHA.
LineRange.title: LineRange

Derivation.description:
How a fact in the output was produced.

Maps directly to a render tag the host LLM can show next to any claim.
Derivation.title: Derivation

Citation.description:
Universal provenance primitive.

Every prose field in PREx output (headlines, one-liners, plan rationales)
carries one or more `Citation` items. The host LLM is expected to render
a fact only via its citation; an unsourced claim is a validation error.

kind.description: Resolution mode for `ref`: 'node' or 'edge' = id in graph.json; 'file_line' = '<path>#L<start>-L<end>' at pr.head_sha; 'external_doc' = absolute URL.
kind.title: Kind

excerpt.description: Optional ≤120-char literal quote of the cited evidence.
excerpt.title: Excerpt

derivation.description: How the claim being cited was produced.

score.description: Optional 0..1 trust score for the claim. Use for visual weighting.
score.title: Score

Citation.title: Citation

Diagnostic.description:
Non-fatal warning emitted during graph or brief construction.

Used to surface static-analysis misses, ambiguity, schema invariant
violations, and LLM enrichment failures. Reviewer-visible at `level`
>= 'warn'; `info` is plumbing.

related_node_ids.description: Node IDs this diagnostic refers to. Empty for global diagnostics.
related_node_ids.title: Related Node Ids

Diagnostic.title: Diagnostic

PRMetadata.description:
Identifying info for the PR the graph was built from.

Captures everything needed to reproduce the parse: repo, base/head SHAs,
branch names, plus surface metadata for UI display. The full PR `body`
is preserved here so the briefing layer can extract author intent.

body.description: Full PR body / description as fetched from the host. Used for intent extraction.
body.title: Body

CallerStub.description:
Lightweight reference to an unchanged symbol that calls/imports a changed one.

Materialised in place of a full `SymbolNode` for symbols outside the changed
file set. Saves bytes; the host LLM can still attach an edge label without
rendering the stub as if it were a peer of changed symbols.

derivation.description: How the resolver derived this node's existence.

score.description: 0..1 trust score. AST/diff sources default to 1.0; LLM-inferred is lower.
score.title: Score

change_state.description: Diff overlay state of this node.

cites.description: Optional citations supporting this node's existence/identity.
cites.title: Cites

file_id.description: ID of the FileNode containing this caller.
file_id.title: File Id

EdgeType.description:
Typed, directed edge.

Allowed source/target kinds per type:
    contains:    Module->File, File->Symbol, File->Hunk, File->CallerStub
    defines:     Hunk->Symbol  (signature-affecting change)
    touches:     Hunk->Symbol  (body-only change)
    calls:       (Symbol|CallerStub)->Symbol
    references:  (Symbol|CallerStub)->Symbol  (non-call: type usage / read)
    imports:     File->(Symbol|Module|CallerStub)
    covers:      Symbol(symbol_kind=test)->Symbol
    external:    Symbol->ExternalRef
EdgeType.title: EdgeType

Edge.description:
Typed directed edge between two nodes.

Each cross-file caller edge MUST carry at least one `Citation` (typically
a `file_line` reference to the call site). Other edges may carry zero.

derivation.description: How this edge was derived.

score.description: 0..1 trust.
score.title: Score

cites.description: Citations grounding this edge — usually file_line references to a call site.
cites.title: Cites

note.description: Free-text annotation. Especially used for llm-derived reasoning.
note.title: Note

Edge.title: Edge

CallerStub.description:
Lightweight reference to an unchanged symbol that calls/imports a changed one.

Materialised in place of a full `SymbolNode` for symbols outside the changed
file set. Saves bytes; the host LLM can still attach an edge label without
rendering the stub as if it were a peer of changed symbols.

id.description: Globally unique within this graph. Format: '<kind>:<stable-key>'.
id.title: Id

derivation.description: How the resolver derived this node's existence.

score.description: 0..1 trust score. AST/diff sources default to 1.0; LLM-inferred is lower.
score.title: Score

change_state.description: Diff overlay state of this node.

cites.description: Optional citations supporting this node's existence/identity.
cites.title: Cites

file_id.description: ID of the FileNode containing this caller.
file_id.title: File Id

CallerStub.title: CallerStub

ReviewStep.description:
One step in the suggested review path.

`what` / `why` / `impact` are LLM-filled iff --llm-summarise. The
deterministic fields (rank, target, risk_signals, cites) are always
populated by the ranking algorithm.

target.description: Primary node id (hunk or symbol) anchoring this step.
target.title: Target

title.description: ≤60-char label of what the step covers.
title.title: Title

what.description: ≤2 sentences. The objective change in plain language.
what.title: What

why.description: ≤2 sentences. Ties to author intent (PR title/body) + pr_type.
why.title: Why

impact.description: ≤2 sentences. Quotes blast_radius numbers + downstream callers.
impact.title: Impact

related_targets.description: Secondary node ids the reviewer should glance at while on this step.
related_targets.title: Related Targets

cites.description: Citations grounding the step. Each prose field must cite at least one node/edge/file_line.
cites.title: Cites

ReviewStep.title: ReviewStep

ReviewPlan.description:
The guided navigation through the PR.

One block, one component group on the host. Triple-framed (What/Why/Impact)
so the host LLM can map each part to a distinct UI affordance.

overview.description: ≤3 sentences tying the steps together as a navigation through-line.
overview.title: Overview

HunkInsight.description:
Per-hunk semantic anchor.

`risk_signals` is AST-derived (deterministic). `one_liner` and `intent`
are filled by the LLM only when --llm-summarise is set.

hunk_id.description: ID of the HunkNode this insight describes.
hunk_id.title: Hunk Id

risk_signals.description: AST-derived risk markers found in the hunk's added/removed lines.
risk_signals.title: Risk Signals

affects_public_symbol_ids.description: Symbol node ids the hunk modifies/defines that are marked public.
affects_public_symbol_ids.title: Affects Public Symbol Ids

cites.description: Citations grounding the insight. At least one required when one_liner is set.
cites.title: Cites

HunkInsight.title: HunkInsight

Citation.description:
Universal provenance primitive.

Every prose field in PREx output (headlines, one-liners, plan rationales)
carries one or more `Citation` items. The host LLM is expected to render
a fact only via its citation; an unsourced claim is a validation error.

kind.description: Resolution mode for `ref`: 'node' or 'edge' = id in graph.json; 'file_line' = '<path>#L<start>-L<end>' at pr.head_sha; 'external_doc' = absolute URL.
kind.title: Kind

excerpt.description: Optional ≤120-char literal quote of the cited evidence.
excerpt.title: Excerpt

derivation.description: How the claim being cited was produced.

score.description: Optional 0..1 trust score for the claim. Use for visual weighting.
score.title: Score

Citation.title: Citation

Derivation.description:
How a fact in the output was produced.

Maps directly to a render tag the host LLM can show next to any claim.
Derivation.title: Derivation

ChecklistItem.description: One review check, bound to specific targets in the graph.

text.description: Human-readable text rendered in the UI.
text.title: Text

targets.description: Hunk or symbol node ids this item applies to.
targets.title: Targets

auto_status.description: Auto-evaluated status when the predicate runs deterministically.
auto_status.title: Auto Status

ChecklistBinding.description: A grouping of checklist items, optionally scoped to a PR type.

type.description: When set, items apply only to PRs of this type. None = generic.
type.title: Type

Diagnostic.description:
Non-fatal warning emitted during graph or brief construction.

Used to surface static-analysis misses, ambiguity, schema invariant
violations, and LLM enrichment failures. Reviewer-visible at `level`
>= 'warn'; `info` is plumbing.

related_node_ids.description: Node IDs this diagnostic refers to. Empty for global diagnostics.
related_node_ids.title: Related Node Ids

Diagnostic.title: Diagnostic

HunkInsight.description:
Per-hunk semantic anchor.

`risk_signals` is AST-derived (deterministic). `one_liner` and `intent`
are filled by the LLM only when --llm-summarise is set.

hunk_id.description: ID of the HunkNode this insight describes.
hunk_id.title: Hunk Id

risk_signals.description: AST-derived risk markers found in the hunk's added/removed lines.
risk_signals.title: Risk Signals

affects_public_symbol_ids.description: Symbol node ids the hunk modifies/defines that are marked public.
affects_public_symbol_ids.title: Affects Public Symbol Ids

cites.description: Citations grounding the insight. At least one required when one_liner is set.
cites.title: Cites

HunkInsight.title: HunkInsight

Novelty.description: Counters that quantify what's new vs touched.
Novelty.title: Novelty

PRMetadata.description:
Identifying info for the PR the graph was built from.

Captures everything needed to reproduce the parse: repo, base/head SHAs,
branch names, plus surface metadata for UI display. The full PR `body`
is preserved here so the briefing layer can extract author intent.

body.description: Full PR body / description as fetched from the host. Used for intent extraction.
body.title: Body

PRMetadata.title: PRMetadata

ReviewBrief.description: PR-level hero block. One block, one component group.

pr_type_evidence.description: Evidence supporting `pr_type`: title prefix, glob match, etc.
pr_type_evidence.title: Pr Type Evidence

ReviewBrief.title: ReviewBrief

ReviewPlan.description:
The guided navigation through the PR.

One block, one component group on the host. Triple-framed (What/Why/Impact)
so the host LLM can map each part to a distinct UI affordance.

overview.description: ≤3 sentences tying the steps together as a navigation through-line.
overview.title: Overview

ReviewPlan.title: ReviewPlan

ReviewStep.description:
One step in the suggested review path.

`what` / `why` / `impact` are LLM-filled iff --llm-summarise. The
deterministic fields (rank, target, risk_signals, cites) are always
populated by the ranking algorithm.

target.description: Primary node id (hunk or symbol) anchoring this step.
target.title: Target

title.description: ≤60-char label of what the step covers.
title.title: Title

what.description: ≤2 sentences. The objective change in plain language.
what.title: What

why.description: ≤2 sentences. Ties to author intent (PR title/body) + pr_type.
why.title: Why

impact.description: ≤2 sentences. Quotes blast_radius numbers + downstream callers.
impact.title: Impact

related_targets.description: Secondary node ids the reviewer should glance at while on this step.
related_targets.title: Related Targets

cites.description: Citations grounding the step. Each prose field must cite at least one node/edge/file_line.
cites.title: Cites

ReviewStep.title: ReviewStep

Brief.description:
Briefing for the generative-UI host LLM.

Sibling of `Graph`. Host LLM consumes `brief.json` first; reaches into
`graph.json` only when following a citation.

graph_ref.description: Relative path to the sibling graph.json artifact.
graph_ref.title: Graph Ref

llm_used.description: True iff at least one prose field was filled by an LLM call.
llm_used.title: Llm Used

Brief.title: Brief
